meta.note: Rest-of-Africa sweep (West/Central, Horn/East, Southern + islands). The remaining African countries have very thin title-level book-ban records — most documented censorship is of press, not specific books. Only solidly-sourced titles included. New country rows added for Cameroon (CM) and Angola (AO). Main basse sur le Cameroun (existing book, had FR ban) gets a CM ban-add; Luuanda (existing, had PT ban) gets an AO ban-add. Held: Senegal/Satanic Verses (single weak Wikipedia line). Most swept countries yielded nothing.

entries.title: Le pauvre Christ de Bomba
entries.description_ban: Satire of French Catholic missionary collusion with colonial power; under pressure from Archbishop René Graffin of Yaoundé the colonial administration banned its distribution in Cameroon.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific colonial-era ban; documented by Britannica and Wikipedia.

entries.title: Main basse sur le Cameroun
entries.description_ban: Essay denouncing the Ahidjo regime as a French neocolonial construct; banned and seized in both France and Cameroon on release. Adds a Cameroon ban to the existing record (which already holds the France ban).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Cameroon ban; documented by Britannica and Wikipedia.

entries.title: A Naked Needle
entries.description_ban: Farah's second novel offended Siad Barre's regime, which condemned the book and banned his works; warned of imprisonment if he returned, Farah remained in exile.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban within Farah's regime-banned oeuvre; documented by Encyclopedia.com and EBSCO.

entries.title: Sweet and Sour Milk
entries.description_ban: First volume of the 'Variations on the Theme of an African Dictatorship' trilogy; its publication confirmed Farah's persona-non-grata status and the Barre regime's ban on his novels, forcing a 22-year exile.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by Encyclopedia.com and EBSCO.

entries.title: Diamantes de Sangue
entries.description_ban: Investigation into killings, torture and corruption in Angola's Lunda diamond fields ('Blood Diamonds'); after Marques's May 2015 conviction a Luanda court ordered him to withdraw the book from the market (including online) and not republish or translate it.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Court-ordered withdrawal of a specific title; documented by MISA and Columbia Global Freedom of Expression.

entries.title: Luuanda
entries.description_ban: Short-story collection of Luanda's musseques; the 1972 edition was judged 'incitement to revolt' and seized in 1973 under Portuguese colonial censorship operating over Angola. Adds an Angola ban to the existing record (which holds the Portugal ban).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds an Angola (colonial-era) ban; documented in Revista Brasileira de História (peer-reviewed).
